pages.raw_text:
(By United Press Leased Wire)


CHICAGO, March 22.-The
Brotherhood of Firemen and En-
ginemen and the railroad general
managers committee today are
considering a compromise measure
submitted to them by Labor Com
missioner NeiII. The railway man.
ayers announced that they do not
expect strike of their firemen.
Decision by both sides today prob
ably will definitely determine the
matter.


w. S. Carter, president of the
Brotherhood, was in session with
committee Of that organization.
Chairman. Nixon of the managers
held a conference with the men,
bers of his committee.


The far reaching effects of a
firemen's strike is shown by the
following outline of the situation
given by a prominent railway Off
cial here:


Number of railroads affected, 40.


Mileage approximately 10000.

fected, $4,000.


Monthly pay of men immediate
ly affected, S1,600,000.


Employees affected: Firemen,
wipers and all yardmen.


Principal railroads affected:
Santa Fe, Northwestern, Chicago
G. Alton, Burlington, Chicago &
Milwaukee & st. Paul, Rock
Island, Colorado & Southern,
Great Northern, Illinois Central,
Missouri, Kansas & Texas, North
ern Pacific, Oregon Short Line,
Oregon Railroad & Navigation,
Frisco, Union Pacific and all
southern lines.


Points in dispute: General wage
increase averaging II IL per cent.
